title: Same-Day Crowns: How They Work and What They Cost
seoTitle: Same-Day Crowns: How CEREC Works and Cost (2026)
seoDescription: How same-day crowns (CEREC) work, what they cost in Texas in 2026, and how they compare to traditional 2-visit crowns on price, durability, and time.
author: Sonria Dentista clinical team
summary: A clear comparison of same-day crowns vs traditional 2-visit crowns: how each one works, real 2026 pricing in Texas, durability differences, and which option makes sense for which patient.

body.text: A cracked tooth on a Tuesday should not mean two weeks of eating soft food and a temporary crown that pops off when you yawn. Same-day crowns, made and fitted in a single visit, have changed how dentists handle broken and worn-down teeth. This guide explains how same-day crowns work, what they cost in Texas in 2026, and how they compare to traditional two-visit crowns on price, longevity, and convenience.
What is a same-day crown?

A same-day crown is a dental crown designed, milled, and cemented in a single appointment, usually two to three hours from start to finish. Most use a CEREC system, which scans your tooth in 3D, designs the crown on a computer, and carves it from a ceramic block in the office while you wait.
There is no putty impression, no temporary crown, and no waiting two weeks for an outside lab to ship the final piece. You walk in with a damaged tooth and walk out with a permanent ceramic one.
How a same-day crown is made, step by step
Here is what actually happens during the visit:
1. Numbing and tooth prep (30 to 45 minutes)
The dentist numbs the area, cleans out any decay, and shapes the tooth so the new crown will fit on top. This is the same step a traditional crown requires.
2. Digital scan (5 to 10 minutes)
Instead of biting into a tray of putty, you sit while a small wand scans the prepared tooth and the surrounding teeth. The scan creates a precise 3D model on the computer.
3. Computer design (15 to 20 minutes)
The dentist designs your new crown on the screen, matching the shape, size, and bite of the original tooth. You can review it before it gets cut.
4. Milling (15 to 30 minutes)
The design is sent to a milling machine in the office. The machine carves your crown out of a single ceramic block. You usually wait in the chair or step out for a coffee.
5. Glazing and bonding (30 to 45 minutes)

The crown is polished, sometimes color-matched with a quick stain, fired in a small oven for hardness, and bonded to your tooth. The dentist checks your bite, makes any final adjustments, and you are done.
Total chair time: 90 minutes to 3 hours, depending on the tooth and the complexity. Plan for half a day.
How does a traditional 2-visit crown work?
The traditional process splits the same work across two visits, two to three weeks apart:

items: Visit 1 (60 to 90 minutes): Numbing, tooth prep, putty impression, temporary crown cemented on. You leave with a temporary that is meant to last 2 to 3 weeks.
Lab time (10 to 14 days): The impression is shipped to an outside lab. A technician casts a model and builds your crown by hand or by lab milling.
Visit 2 (30 to 45 minutes): Temporary off, final crown bonded on, bite checked, done.

body.text: Same-day crown vs traditional crown: a side-by-side comparison
Time

Same-day: one visit, 2 to 3 hours. Traditional: two visits, 90 minutes for visit 1, then 30 minutes for visit 2, with 2 to 3 weeks of waiting and a temporary in between. If you are a working parent, the time savings is significant.
Cost

In Texas in 2026, both options are usually priced about the same: $1,000 to $1,800 per crown for cash patients, $200 to $700 for insured patients after coverage. Some clinics charge $100 to $200 more for same-day, but many price them identically because the in-office lab eliminates the outside lab fee.
Materials

Same-day crowns are almost always all-ceramic, usually lithium disilicate or zirconia. Traditional crowns can be ceramic too, but also offer porcelain-fused-to-metal and full gold options, which are still preferred for some back-tooth cases. For visible teeth, both options look natural.
Durability

Long-term studies through 2024 show same-day ceramic crowns last 10 to 15 years on average, similar to lab-made ceramic crowns. Lab-made gold crowns can last longer (20 plus years) but most patients do not want gold on a visible tooth. The biggest predictors of crown lifespan are not the manufacturing method but how well you brush, floss, and avoid grinding.
Fit and accuracy
Modern digital scans are at least as accurate as putty impressions, often more so. The biggest variable is the dentist's skill in tooth prep and bite adjustment, not the crown manufacturing method.
Color matching

Lab-made crowns can be hand-stained and layered for very subtle color, which matters for front teeth in patients with complex tooth coloring. Same-day crowns are excellent for back teeth and most front teeth, but for a single front tooth on someone with very specific shading, a lab-made crown can sometimes look slightly more natural.
Who is a good candidate for a same-day crown?
Most patients are. Same-day crowns work well for:

items: Replacing an old metal crown with a tooth-colored one.
Teeth that have had a root canal and need protection.
Patients with a busy schedule who cannot easily come back twice.

body.text: Some patients still benefit from a traditional crown:

items: Very complex front-tooth cases where color matching has to be done by hand.
Heavy grinders who would benefit from a metal-based crown for back teeth.
Cases that need significant gum work first before the impression can be taken cleanly.

body.text: How much do same-day crowns cost in 2026?
In Texas in 2026, the cash price for a same-day ceramic crown ranges from $1,000 to $1,800. The wide range reflects:

items: Location: Dallas and Houston metros run higher than smaller towns.
Material: Standard ceramic vs premium zirconia or lithium disilicate.
Tooth position: Molars sometimes cost slightly more due to the harder ceramic blocks used.
Whether the clinic charges separately for the digital scan and bonding visit.

body.text: If you have a PPO dental plan, crowns are usually covered at 50% after deductible, with a yearly maximum of $1,000 to $2,500 limiting how much insurance pays. A typical out-of-pocket for an insured patient is $300 to $700.

body.title: Watch for these add-ons
body.text: Some clinics quote a low base crown price but charge separately for: 3D scan ($75 to $150), build-up under the crown ($150 to $350), and seating visit ($75). Always ask for an all-inclusive quote in writing.
How long do same-day crowns last?
Average lifespan in 2026 data: 10 to 15 years. Some last 20 plus. The main causes of failure:

items: Decay around the edge of the crown (your home brushing matters more than the crown).
Grinding and clenching without a night guard.
Trauma (sports injury, biting on a fork).
The underlying tooth fracturing below the crown.

body.text: If your dentist gives you a 5-year warranty on the crown itself, that is reasonable industry standard. We replace any same-day crown that fails for non-trauma reasons within 5 years, and we discuss this in writing before treatment.
Frequently asked questions
Does insurance cover same-day crowns the same as traditional crowns?
Yes. Insurance pays based on the procedure code (D2740 for a porcelain crown), not on whether it was made same-day or in a lab. Coverage is usually 50% after deductible.
Can a same-day crown break the same day?
Very rare in the first 24 hours. The bonding cure is full strength within 10 minutes. We recommend skipping anything sticky or extremely hard for the first 24 hours, just to be safe.
What if my tooth needs a root canal first?

Then the visit splits in two. Root canal first (90 minutes), then the same-day crown after, either later that day or at a separate appointment. Some offices prefer to do the crown the next day so the tooth has time to settle.
Will the color match my other teeth?
For back teeth, almost always. For front teeth, the dentist picks a shade and stains the crown to match. If you are unhappy with the match before bonding, we adjust before final cement.
Book a crown consult

If you have a cracked or worn-down tooth and want to know if a same-day crown is right for you, we offer free crown consultations at our Arlington and Grand Prairie offices, both equipped with in-office milling. Call (682) 284-1120 to book.
